name: Swimming
description: A water locomotion state with altered control, buoyancy, visibility, and resource rules.
design_notes: Swimming should feel like a distinct environment rather than a slower copy of walking. Decide whether water is safe traversal, a survival pressure, a stealth route, or a puzzle space. Good swimming entries define surface transitions, oxygen rules, camera behavior, and what actions remain available underwater.
design_purpose: Change navigation and encounter possibilities when the player enters water.
Create environmental contrast through buoyancy, drag, oxygen, and visibility.
Support underwater routes, secrets, hazards, or survival pressure.
player_fantasy: The player feels adapted to a different medium, moving with buoyancy and restraint instead of normal ground control.

parameters.name: swim_stroke_impulse
parameters.description: Impulse applied when the player performs an active swim stroke.

parameters.name: water_drag_coefficient
parameters.description: Drag applied to horizontal and vertical motion in water.

parameters.name: surface_snap_depth
parameters.description: Depth below the surface that allows transition to surface swimming.

parameters.name: oxygen_depletion_per_second
parameters.description: Oxygen cost while submerged if the game tracks breath.

edge_cases: Entering water at high velocity should decide whether to preserve momentum, splash-stop, or damage the player.
Surface, ladder, ledge, and shore transitions need priority rules to avoid input traps.
Camera clipping and target lock need special handling when the water surface cuts through the view.
Swimming out of bounds or under moving geometry can strand the player if exit checks are too strict.
common_bugs: The player repeatedly enters and exits swim state at the water surface due to noisy volume overlap.
Gravity and buoyancy are both applied after the state exits, causing a sudden launch.
Oxygen continues draining in shallow water or during pause because breath state is not tied to submersion.
Grounded actions remain available underwater and break animation or combat assumptions.
balancing_notes: Low drag makes water a fast route; high drag makes it a hazard or puzzle medium.
Oxygen pressure should be paired with readable exits, refills, or safe pockets.
accessibility_notes: Use distinct visual and audio cues for oxygen warnings before damage begins.
Provide camera and motion options for underwater distortion or bobbing effects.

implementation_notes.unity: Use an explicit movement-state enum and feed the final desired velocity into CharacterController.Move or Rigidbody movement from FixedUpdate; keep dash and jump assists outside animation callbacks. For Swimming, validate this path with parameters such as swim_stroke_impulse and water_drag_coefficient.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep the mechanic state on CharacterBody2D or CharacterBody3D, update velocity in _physics_process, and separate input buffering from collision response. For Swimming, validate this path with parameters such as swim_stroke_impulse and water_drag_coefficient.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use CharacterMovement with a custom movement state or Gameplay Ability; gate activation through prediction-safe input and reconcile movement corrections cleanly in networked play. For Swimming, validate this path with parameters such as swim_stroke_impulse and water_drag_coefficient.
implementation_notes.web: Represent the mechanic as deterministic state in the simulation loop, then apply resolved displacement before rendering through Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or a custom engine. For Swimming, validate this path with parameters such as swim_stroke_impulse and water_drag_coefficient.

relationships.reason: Swimming depends on fluid volumes, submerged ratio, drag, and buoyancy state for believable movement.
Underwater swimming can spend oxygen and escalate into survival pressure when submerged too long.
Long swims often drain stamina so distance, currents, and safe exits matter.

example_games.title: Subnautica
example_games.note: Uses swimming as primary underwater traversal with visible oxygen pressure.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
example_games.note: Lets Link swim across water while stamina limits longer crossings.

example_games.title: Minecraft
example_games.note: Uses swimming and underwater movement with visible breath pressure.

example_games.title: Super Metroid
example_games.note: Uses underwater movement as a distinct traversal condition in exploration spaces.
